examples.title: Pure ALOHA Simulation
examples.description: Simulates Pure ALOHA protocol to calculate throughput for different traffic loads
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PureALOHA_Simulation
BEGIN
 INPUT: num_frames, frame_time, traffic_load G
 OUTPUT: Throughput S

 SET successful = 0
 SET collision = 0
 SET time_slots = num_frames * 10
 SET frame_attempts = empty list

 FOR i = 1 to num_frames
 SET attempt_time = RANDOM(0, time_slots)
 INSERT attempt_time INTO frame_attempts
 END FOR

 FOR each frame in frame_attempts
 SET vulnerable_start = frame.attempt_time - frame_time
 SET vulnerable_end = frame.attempt_time + frame_time

 SET has_collision = FALSE
 FOR each other_frame in frame_attempts
 IF other_frame.attempt_time != frame.attempt_time THEN
 IF other_frame.attempt_time >= vulnerable_start AND
 other_frame.attempt_time <= vulnerable_end THEN
 has_collision = TRUE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR

 IF has_collision = TRUE THEN
 collision = collision + 1
 ELSE
 successful = successful + 1
 END IF
 END FOR

 S = successful / num_frames
 PRINT "Throughput:", S
 PRINT "Theoretical Max:", 0.184, "at G = 0.5"
END
examples.explanation: This simulation generates random frame transmission times and checks for collisions within the vulnerable period (2T). It counts successful transmissions vs collisions to compute actual throughput. The theoretical maximum of 18.4% occurs when G=0.5 frames per frame time.

examples.title: CSMA/CD Backoff Algorithm
examples.description: Implements binary exponential backoff algorithm used in Ethernet after collision detection
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CSMA_CD_Backoff
BEGIN
 INPUT: collision_count, max_retries
 OUTPUT: wait_time (in slot times)

 SET k = MIN(collision_count, 10)
 SET slot_range = 2^k - 1
 SET wait_time = RANDOM(0, slot_range)

 PRINT "Collision number:", collision_count
 PRINT "Backoff exponent k:", k
 PRINT "Slot range: 0 to", slot_range
 PRINT "Random wait time:", wait_time, "slot times"

 IF collision_count > max_retries THEN
 PRINT "Frame discarded after", max_retries, "attempts"
 RETURN -1
 ELSE
 WAIT(wait_time * slot_time)
 RETURN wait_time
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM Ethernet_Transmission
BEGIN
 SET collision_count = 0
 SET max_retries = 16
 SET slot_time = 51.2 microseconds (for 10Mbps Ethernet)

 WHILE TRUE DO
 SENSING: Check if carrier is present
 IF carrier IS idle THEN
 TRANSMIT frame
 WHILE transmitting DO
 IF collision_detected THEN
 SEND jam_signal
 collision_count = collision_count + 1
 wait_time = CSMA_CD_Backoff(collision_count, max_retries)
 IF wait_time == -1 THEN
 BREAK
 END IF
 GOTO SENSING
 END IF
 END WHILE
 PRINT "Frame transmitted successfully"
 BREAK
 ELSE
 WAIT until carrier idle
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: The backoff algorithm randomly selects a wait time from 0 to 2^n-1 slot times after nth collision. This doubling of range (binary exponential) reduces probability of repeated collisions. After 16 retries, the frame is discarded.

examples.title: CSMA/CA WiFi Access Mechanism
examples.description: Implements the DCF (Distributed Coordination Function) in WiFi with RTS/CTS option
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WiFi_Channel_Access
BEGIN
 INPUT: frame_size, use_rts_cts
 SET DIFS = 34 microseconds (for 802.11b)
 SET SIFS = 10 microseconds
 SET slot_time = 20 microseconds
 SET CW_MIN = 31
 SET CW_MAX = 1023

 SENSING: Check channel idle/busy (physical + NAV)
 IF channel_busy THEN
 WAIT until channel idle
 WAIT DIFS
 SET NAV = 0
 END IF

 IF use_rts_cts THEN
 SEND RTS (Request to Send)
 WAIT SIFS
 IF CTS received THEN
 SEND Data
 WAIT SIFS
 SEND ACK
 ELSE
 GOTO BACKOFF
 END IF
 ELSE
 SEND Data
 WAIT SIFS
 SEND ACK
 END IF

 BACKOFF:
 SET CW = CW_MIN
 SET backoff_slots = RANDOM(0, CW)

 WHILE backoff_slots > 0 DO
 IF channel_idle THEN
 WAIT slot_time
 backoff_slots = backoff_slots - 1
 ELSE
 WAIT until channel idle
 WAIT DIFS
 backoff_slots = backoff_slots - 1
 END IF
 END WHILE

 TRANSMIT
END

ALGORITHM NAV_SetFromRTS
BEGIN
 INPUT: rts_frame
 SET duration = rts_frame.duration
 SET my_NAV = duration
 PRINT "NAV set to:", duration, "(from RTS)"
 DECREMENT NAV over time
END
examples.explanation: WiFi uses CSMA/CA with random backoff after DIFS. For large frames, RTS/CTS exchange reserves channel. NAV (Network Allocation Vector) is set from duration fields in RTS/CTS/DATA/ACK frames, allowing stations to know when channel will be busy without hearing all transmissions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is backoff window
